subject: Chemistry
title: Supercooled Water: Instant Ice
description: Learn Supercooled Water: Instant Ice in one minute.

hook.text: Pour perfectly still water below 0°C and it stays liquid. Tap the bottle and it freezes instantly.

buildup.text: Water needs a nucleation site—a tiny impurity or vibration—to start forming ice crystals.

discovery.text: Without a nucleus, water molecules can't organize into ice. They remain liquid well below freezing.

twist.text: Trees use antifreeze proteins to survive winter. They prevent nucleation inside their cells.

climax.text: Understanding supercooling helps us preserve organs for transplant and predict ice formation on aircraft.

punchline.text: Liquid below zero. One tap and it freezes solid. Nucleation is the trigger.

quiz.question: Why does supercooled water stay liquid below 0°C?
options: It lacks a nucleation site to start crystal formation
It contains dissolved salt that lowers the freezing point
The container insulates it from the cold
